claim: Context engineering principle: LLMs are stateless and non-deterministic; better token input leads to better token output - optimize context window for correctness, completeness, size, and trajectory
actionable: Treat context management as core engineering discipline; structure conversations to maintain information quality and avoid noise that pollutes model decision-making
source_title: No Vibes Allowed: Solving Hard Problems in Complex Codebases – Dex Horthy, HumanLayer

claim: Research-Plan-Implement workflow with intentional compaction: compress context into markdown between phases to avoid entering the 'dumb zone' (40%+ of context window)
actionable: Use research-plan-implement cycle with explicit compaction: (1) Research what system does, (2) Create detailed plan with code snippets, (3) Implement plan; restart context at phase boundaries
source_title: No Vibes Allowed: Solving Hard Problems in Complex Codebases – Dex Horthy, HumanLayer

claim: Coding agents perform poorly beyond 40% context window utilization ('dumb zone'); maintaining low context utilization is critical for reliable code generation
actionable: Keep active context under 40% of available window; use sub-agents or compaction to offload reading/searching; focus remaining space on decision-making
source_title: No Vibes Allowed: Solving Hard Problems in Complex Codebases – Dex Horthy, HumanLayer

claim: Sub-agents should control context, not model roles; use sub-agents to fork context windows for codebase exploration, returning succinct findings to parent
actionable: Design sub-agent systems to isolate expensive operations (file searching, reading) in separate context windows; parent agent remains in smart zone for decision-making
source_title: No Vibes Allowed: Solving Hard Problems in Complex Codebases – Dex Horthy, HumanLayer

claim: Do not outsource thinking: AI amplifies thinking you have done; bad research leads to bad plans which lead to 100x bad lines of code
actionable: Maintain human judgment in research and planning phases; review agent-generated plans before implementation; poor plan design cascades into massive code mistakes
source_title: No Vibes Allowed: Solving Hard Problems in Complex Codebases – Dex Horthy, HumanLayer

claim: Plans with actual code snippets provide reliable execution; too-long plans reduce readability, too-short plans reduce reliability; find optimal balance per team
actionable: Include specific code snippets in plans showing exact changes; iterate to find plan length that balances readability with execution reliability for your team
source_title: No Vibes Allowed: Solving Hard Problems in Complex Codebases – Dex Horthy, HumanLayer

claim: Progressive disclosure of codebase context: put high-level docs at root, add layer-specific context deeper in hierarchy to avoid context explosion
actionable: Structure codebase docs with progressive disclosure: root-level overview + directory-specific context at each level; agents pull relevant context dynamically
source_title: No Vibes Allowed: Solving Hard Problems in Complex Codebases – Dex Horthy, HumanLayer

claim: Stanford study: most extra code shipped by AI tools is rework from previous week; brownfield codebases suffer more than greenfield; context engineering solves this
actionable: Recognize AI tool limitations in complex codebases; implement context engineering workflows (research-plan-implement) to reduce rework and maintain code quality
source_title: No Vibes Allowed: Solving Hard Problems in Complex Codebases – Dex Horthy, HumanLayer
